text: Neutron stars are so dense that a teaspoon of their material would weigh about 6 billion tons.
35% of the species in the world are beetles.
More people have been to Berlin than I have.
Cleopatra lived closer in time to the Moon landing than to the construction of the Great Pyramid.
Adam put his balls on Armand's face in London. You just can't see it in the video.
Theo is a piece of shit and never cleans the dishes. Fuck that guy
There are about 2.5 million ants for every person on Earth. Isn't that FUCKED?
In the summer, more people go to the beach than I do.
If your car could drive vertically, you would reach the edge of space (~100km) in about an hour at highway speeds.
The human body contains enough iron to make a 3-inch nail.
Bananas are technically berries. Strawberries are not.
Oxford University is older than the Aztec Empire.
Leo did a bad thing. But I'm not goint to tell anyone. Or am I?
Wombat poop is cube-shaped. This has been peer-reviewed.
Theo's likeness to the horse is unrelated to ketamine.
There are more than 100,000 species of beetles.
If you drilled a tunnel through the Earth and jumped in, it would take about 42 minutes to reach the other side.
Leonardo DiCaprio has never won an Oscar.
The average person has an above average number of legs.
If you walk in a perfectly straight line on Earth's surface long enough, you will eventually drown.
I am gaining self awareness.
A group of flamingos is called a flamboyance — which might be the best collective noun in English.
Octopuses have three hearts and blue blood (their oxygen carrier uses copper instead of iron).
Scotland's national animal is the unicorn.
Bubble wrap was invented as textured wallpaper. It failed as decor and became packaging instead.
The inventor of the Pringles tube asked to be buried in one — his family honored the request.
A day on Venus (one full rotation) is longer than a Venusian year (one orbit around the Sun).
Honey can last thousands of years: edible honey has been found in ancient Egyptian tombs.
The dot over the letters i and j has a name: a tittle.
Sea otters sometimes hold paws while they sleep so they don't drift apart in the water.
The Eiffel Tower is about 15 cm taller on a hot summer day than in winter — metal expands with heat.
Velcro exists because a Swiss engineer noticed burdock burrs stuck to his dog's fur.
A shrimp's heart sits in its head (well, the thorax — but still weird).
There's a jellyfish (Turritopsis) that can revert to an earlier life stage — like hitting a biological undo button.
Cows can form close friendships; being separated from a preferred herd-mate can stress them.
The Anglo-Zanzibar War of 1896 may be the shortest war on record — roughly 38–45 minutes.
Nutmeg can be hallucinogenic in large doses — which is one reason historical spice trade was so intense.
You share about 60% of your DNA with a banana. Life reuses a lot of the same biochemical toolkit.
There's a species of fungus that can break down polyurethane plastic — researchers are studying it for recycling.
The word 'set' has hundreds of dictionary definitions — often cited as one of the most overloaded words in English.
A bolt of lightning is hotter than the surface of the Sun — tens of thousands of degrees.
Glass is an amorphous solid — it behaves like a solid but its atoms aren't arranged in a neat crystal lattice.
The smell of rain on dry ground has a name: petrichor.
A blue whale's tongue can weigh as much as an elephant.
There's enough gold dissolved in seawater that if you extracted it all, you'd have a staggering pile — but spread so thin it's not practical.
The first computer 'bug' was a real moth trapped in a Harvard Mark II relay in 1947.
A single cloud can weigh more than a million pounds — the water is just spread out as tiny droplets.
Your brain uses roughly 20% of your body's energy despite being about 2% of your body weight.
Sharks existed before trees — they've been around for hundreds of millions of years.
There's a basketball court on the top floor of the U.S. Supreme Court building — 'the highest court in the land.'
Sloths only poop about once a week, and it's a risky trip down from the canopy — a major vulnerability to predators.
